weather.tip: Overcast evening at 25.7°C with a 29% rain chance — today's forecasts have flagged intense thundershowers disrupting traffic, so keep a rain layer handy and add buffer time if you're heading out tonight.

around.headline: Bengaluru law student stabbed to death by ex-boyfriend's brother after she ended the relationship
around.summary: A law student was killed after she ended things with a man who had concealed an existing marriage from her; his brother allegedly stabbed her in apparent retaliation. Anyone facing persistent unwanted contact or threats from an ex should document it and involve police or the women's helpline early, rather than waiting for it to escalate.

around.headline: Man strangles pregnant wife with a dog leash in South Bengaluru, then dies by suicide
around.summary: A pregnant woman was strangled to death by her husband, who then killed himself, in a South Bengaluru flat. Anyone experiencing or aware of domestic violence can reach the Karnataka Women's Helpline (181) or police (112) — intervention doesn't have to wait for a crisis point.

around.headline: Three vehicles damaged in a Hebbal flyover crash, traffic snarled during peak hours
around.summary: A crash on the Hebbal flyover damaged three vehicles and held up peak-hour traffic in the area. If you regularly use the Hebbal stretch, build in extra time this evening and check live traffic apps before setting out.

around.headline: UPI glitch on BMTC buses: passengers pay but tickets don't print
around.summary: A payment glitch is causing some BMTC UPI transactions to go through without a ticket being issued. Keep a screenshot of the payment confirmation as proof if a conductor's machine fails to print your ticket.

around.headline: Karnataka High Court tells apartment associations to rethink curbs on kids playing on premises
around.summary: The High Court has asked apartment complexes to reconsider rules that restrict children from playing within the premises, affirming it as a right. Residents in gated communities with similar play-area restrictions now have a legal precedent to cite if they want the rules revisited.

around.headline: US strikes on Iran escalate, with Iran reportedly telling Houthis to threaten the Red Sea shipping route
around.summary: The US-Iran conflict has widened, with reports of Iran asking Houthi forces to close the Red Sea shipping gateway in retaliation. A prolonged Middle East shipping disruption tends to push up crude oil and freight costs, which can eventually show up at Indian fuel pumps and in import prices.

events.name: Ranga Shankara's global children's festival
events.note: A festival aimed at getting kids engaged beyond screens, with performances and activities — worth it if you're looking for a screen-free outing with children.

events.name: Mahesh Kale's Abhangwari concert
events.note: A classical vocal concert of Marathi devotional abhangs by Mahesh Kale — a good pick for a classical-music evening.

events.name: Channapatna Craft Experience at NGMA Bengaluru
events.note: A hands-on showcase of Channapatna's traditional wooden-craft techniques at the Art & Craft Experience Centre — a good pick if you want to see or try a heritage craft.

events.name: Nouvelle Vague live in Bengaluru
events.note: The world-touring French band known for bossa-nova covers of new-wave classics makes a Bengaluru stop — worth it for a different kind of gig night.

street_smart.title: Your ex won't stop messaging you and has shown up outside your workplace twice this week after you ended things over a lie he told. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real case reported today: a Bengaluru law student was stabbed to death by her ex-boyfriend's brother after she ended the relationship upon discovering he had concealed an existing marriage from her (The Times of India, NDTV, Telangana Today).
street_smart.situation: You dated someone for several months before discovering he had hidden a previous marriage from you. You ended it and blocked his number, but he keeps contacting you from new numbers, and this week he has shown up outside your college or office twice. What do you do?

choices.text: Ignore it and hope it fades on its own — making it a 'big deal' might provoke him further.
choices.feedback: Ignoring escalating unwanted contact is exactly the pattern that precedes cases like this week's — repeated messaging from new numbers and showing up at your workplace are warning signs that tend to escalate, not fade, when left unaddressed. Documenting and reporting early is what actually reduces risk.

choices.text: Save every message and screenshot as evidence, tell your family, friends and workplace/college security what's happening, and file a written complaint at the nearest police station or via the Karnataka Women's Helpline (181).
choices.feedback: Correct. A written, dated record of the contact is what gives police something concrete to act on if things escalate, and it doesn't require waiting for a physical incident first. Making people around you aware — family, friends, workplace security — also means you're not the only one tracking the risk.

choices.text: Confront him yourself in person to tell him to stop, without telling anyone else where or when you're meeting him.
choices.feedback: A solo, unwitnessed confrontation with someone already displaying stalking behavior removes your safety net exactly when you might need it. If you do confront him, do it in a public place in daylight and tell someone your plan and location in advance.

choices.text: Post his name and photo on social media to publicly warn others about him.
choices.feedback: Public callouts occasionally deter, but they can also inflame an already unstable situation and expose you to a defamation counter-complaint. A police complaint carries actual legal weight and protection options that a social post doesn't.

takeaways: Save every message, missed call and screenshot from the moment unwanted contact starts — it's the evidence a police complaint or protection order will need.
Karnataka's Women's Helpline (181) and local police stations take stalking/harassment complaints even before physical harm occurs — you don't have to wait for it to get 'bad enough.'
Tell people around you — family, friends, workplace or college security — as soon as a pattern of unwanted contact starts, so someone besides you is aware of the risk.

quiz.q: Namma Metro's Yellow Line this week received its 14th delivered trainset, with one more still pending — what does a 'trainset delivery' milestone typically mean for a metro line?
options: The line is immediately open to the public
New trains are being readied ahead of trials before the line can open commercially
The line's construction has just begun
Fares for the line have been finalised
quiz.why: Metro lines usually take delivery of their full fleet and run trial operations for months before commercial launch, so 'trains delivered' and 'line opened' are announced as separate milestones.

quiz.q: ISRO issued an internal memo this week to curb an exodus of scientists and engineers from which mission?
quiz.why: Gaganyaan is India's crewed spaceflight programme, aiming to send Indian astronauts to orbit on an indigenously built rocket and capsule — retaining specialised staff is critical to programmes like this that depend on years of accumulated expertise.

quiz.q: PM Modi is set to launch India's first hydrogen-powered train from which state?
quiz.why: Hydrogen trains use fuel cells that convert hydrogen into electricity on board, emitting only water vapour — a cleaner option for routes that aren't electrified rather than running diesel locomotives.

quiz.q: Bengaluru's relatively moderate climate, despite being close to the tropics, owes a lot to its elevation on the Deccan Plateau, roughly how high above sea level?
quiz.why: Sitting at roughly 900 metres keeps temperatures noticeably cooler than most Indian plains cities at similar latitude, which is part of why Bengaluru's summers rarely reach the extremes seen in cities like Delhi or Nagpur.

quiz.q: When a government spends more in a year than it collects in revenue, that gap is called a ______.
quiz.why: A fiscal deficit is about a government's own income versus its spending (usually covered by borrowing), which is a different concept from a trade deficit — that's about a country's imports versus its exports with another country.
